answer: The Bṛhaspati Smṛti (unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070) has the most detailed treatment of legal procedures around debt. The text contains an entire dedicated section (1.10: 'The Recovery of Debt') with multiple subsections covering: (1) the consideration of interest rates (1.10.1), (2) six different kinds of interest—bodily, periodical, compound, stipulated, crest-interest, and interest from enjoyment (1.10.2), (3) pledges and mortgages (1.10.6), and (4) sureties and their obligations (1.10.7). The text also discusses loan documents, debt instruments, witness testimony in debt cases, and the legal procedures for debt recovery. While other Sanskrit Smṛti texts like the Kātyāyana Smṛti address debt-related matters, they do so with less comprehensive coverage. The Bṛhaspati Smṛti's single chapter contains 1,220 paragraphs of detailed legal exposition, with approximately 24 of the top 30 search results for 'debt' coming from this work alone.
